title: PERMANANT brow ptosis from a cannula？! Temple filler safety tips & cannula advice.
filename: PERMANANT brow ptosis from a cannula？! Temple filler safety tips & cannula advice..en.vtt
excerpt: A permanent brow drop from Temple filler recently I came across an awful injury that a patient suffered from a temple revolumize this procedure was done with a canul and the patient suffered immediate brow asymmetry that we thought would recover but unfortunately just persisted n…

segments.text: a permanent brow drop from Temple filler recently I came across an awful injury that a patient suffered from a temple revolumize this procedure was done with a canul and the patient suffered immediate brow asymmetry that we thought would recover but unfortunately just persisted now several months in the patient still does not have movement on one side so how could this happen I think there's a lot to unpack with how canulas can potentially affect patients when used in the temple region and we can compare and contrast this with what might happen or the likelihood of what might happen using this so-called gunshot technique so let's think about the anatomy of the frontal branch of the facial nerve so this nerve emerges from underneath your zygoma it passes through the master muscle comes up through on top of the fascia of the muscle and then makes its way up into the temple now when it makes his way into the temple it is coming up through the layers and it eventually will pass into where many people will place a

canula to revolumize and this is why it's actually quite common to have some effect on the nerve but it's usually temporary so the facial nerve is even sometimes injured on purpose there was a treatment coined frotox many years ago which involved freezing that area so you can actually cool the nerve down by placing a probe into this area for long enough that you actually cause a neuropraxia which means the nerve no longer causes the frontalis muscle to contract and you get an improvement in lines and wrinkles in exactly the same way as you might with a boty toxin treatment if you do the canul technique in temples you'll be relieved to find that in most cases if you get brow heaviness it's actually quite common and it is temporary and it's simply an effect of the lidocane so in the space between the Deep And The Superficial fascia where the nerve resides is exactly where the lidocaine is spreading out and affecting the nerve and this actually happens in a large proportion of treatments so it is quite common unless you tell your patient for them to suddenly spot their eyebrows aren't moving as much and this causes a lot of

concern and anxiety if you haven't warned your patient now the good news is most of the time this will simply wear off in a matter of hours in some cases it can be as long as 8 hours after the procedure before this fully wears off but in other cases like this case I recently came across something else has happened to cause a much longer effect so in most cases this is temporary because the lidocaine eventually wears off all that lidocain does is block the sodium channels on the nerves which means the impulse simply doesn't get through as soon as the lidocaine disperses you get activity back in the muscle now there's another type of neuropraxia that can be caused when the canul damages the nerve itself so if you're unlucky enough to prod or pull um or compress a nerve you can temporarily stop that nerve from working now this first type of injury is exactly the same thing as you may have experienced if you spend too long on your phone on the toilet where you compress the nerve in your leg and end up causing a dead leg which then gets pins and needles when you move on

you move on so the same thing can happen with nerves in the face a compression affects its function which causes a temporary decrease in nerve activity or in neuropraxia and this is probably the most common type of nerve injury that you see after dermal fillers particularly with canular and although it's not that common um you can usually reassure your patient one of the key things I always look for in these cases is how long did it take for that weakness to occur if the muscle weakness occurred immediately during the procedure rather than a few minutes or hours after the procedure it's more likely an injury to the nerve that's more severe than if it came on slowly if you think about how neur neuropraxis generally work it takes a bit of time compressing the nerve before you get the outcome so a compressed nerve is less bad than a severed nerve and a severed nerve would give you a more immediate change in the muscle activity in that area now of course continuous compression can still cause a long lasting neuropraxia and this is another

issue to be aware of if you were to cause compression and not release that compression you can cause a much more long-lasting injury and trying to separate these two out is impossible without allowing time to pass through but the most common cause is the lidocaine followed by compression that's temporary sometimes by the swelling of the procedure and then rarest of all would be a complete severing of the nerve that is essentially permanent so recently anecdotally I've noticed a significant uptick in complications from temples and of course we never know without doing proper studies whether this is just random or whether it's to do with more people doing the procedures um but I'm quite interested in the differences between these two different techniques I know there are certain schools of thought that think for example The Gunshot technique is particularly dangerous and they will use canula for everything and feel very nervous at the thought of doing The Gunshot technique whereas there are also people on the other side of the fence who feel the same way about canular now in my experience and in my clinic in

skin beaver and skin Beaver training we use both techniques and I have seen now three different complications caused by a canula um one of which was compression of the vein in that area which caused uh a swelling and protruding veins which ended up with the clinician having to reverse the procedure that was done with a canula I've also seen two vast reclusions of The Superficial Temple archery with clinicians using the canul technique and I've yet to see any complications from injecting with the gunshot technique now the I clearly do not have a large enough sample but I'm very interested if there's anyone out there who has seen complications from either technique I'd really appreciate it if you would inbox me on Instagram so that we can learn more about what actually plays out so let's talk more about why this longlasting weakness of the face could have occurred after a canular treatment now when you see canular treatments done it's quite clear that you cover a much larger area with your instrument than you do for example with the gunshot technique so moving a large instrument in and out of that

layer you're much more likely to get involved with the nerve and to poke or prod or potentially if you're relatively rough you might even tear or break a nerve so this these are the two types of injuries which I'm most worried about because if you cause compression of the nerve at least that's relatively temporary and it might get better within weeks um but if you tear or twang the nerve to such a degree that it's damaged more severely that's this is called axonic misis then you may have at least months but probably a permanent injury and I think the crucial Factor here is these large instruments which we believe are safer in terms of avoiding blood vessels being put into different parts of the area relatively roughly in different people's hands or just through bad luck you can end up with a more significant injury to these very tiny nerves in that area so what would I do to avoid it assuming I was going to use a canul technique like I always say the analog variables are the hardest ones to measure because how do we measure gentle versus rough it's very subjective

and no one's really able to put a number on that but I do think if you inject as if you're almost like you're diffusing a bomb you know tiny smooth movements and if you get any resistance stop and try and find an easier path through and you're very gentle with it and once you you've opened up the area with a little bit of filler so that the canula slides more easily then you can pass in and out with without putting any pressure on additional structures so for me that's the main thing that will allow fewer patients to suffer negative side effects is just being extra gentle when you're using a canul in this area if you'd like to learn much more about the anatomy and develop real confidence with where these structures lie and how to inject how to make your own decisions without learning just by copying other people then make sure you sign up in the link below to something very exciting I've got coming soon [Music]
